failure_modes.text: Research can devolve into confirmation-seeking when ideas already have champions and the study is framed to protect them.
failure_modes.mitigation: Protect research integrity: when ideas already have champions, design the study to test disconfirming hypotheses, not just to collect evidence for the preferred direction.

failure_modes.text: Availability-style hammer use can force every problem into a familiar research template instead of matching the method to the real uncertainty.
failure_modes.mitigation: Match the method to the uncertainty: diagnose what the team actually needs to learn before selecting a research template, and switch methods when the familiar one does not fit.

failure_modes.text: Over-analysis can waste discovery effort on trivia rather than producing an actionable design imperative.
failure_modes.mitigation: Focus on decision-relevant findings: set an actionability threshold before starting research — if a finding would not change the design, it is not worth pursuing.

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.text: What user evidence would genuinely disconfirm the current product story rather than merely refine it?
premortem_questions.source_quote: disciplined search for disconfirming user evidence

heuristics.text: Observe, interview, and immerse before abstracting so the team can see workarounds and unmet needs directly.
Use extreme users to surface non-obvious behaviors that average-user assumptions would miss.
Translate research into empathy maps, journey maps, and design imperatives so evidence changes decisions instead of remaining a report artifact.
heuristics.source_quote: Empathy Maps ... Journey Maps ... Design Imperatives

curation_notes.summary: Kept the intervention layer close to direct observation, extreme-user discovery, and evidence that is strong enough to change a product decision.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broad research-ops overhead because the raw source is strongest on disconfirming evidence and concrete synthesis artifacts.
open_questions: The third premortem question is reviewed compression but remains directly grounded in the source's anti-validation-theater warning.

allies: The source is especially rich on systems thinking, storytelling, and cognitive-load-aware synthesis.
antagonists: The raw source explicitly names confirmation-bias, authority-bias, and motivated reasoning pressure.
structured_tensions: The source explicitly preserves tensions against confirmation-bias and authority-bias.
